verdict: Use Open-Meteo as the sole live weather engine for today's demo: it is keyless, verified working this session for an Indian district (lat 23.2, lon 77.08 near Bhopal) with 16-day daily precipitation_sum, et0_fao_evapotranspiration, temp max/min, precipitation probability, hourly soil moisture, AND past_days=30 lookback in one call — everything needed to compute both dry-spell (backward-looking) and heavy-rain (forward-looking) zone alerts. IMD's real APIs (city forecast, current weather, nowcast, district warnings) all exist at mausam.imd.gov.in/city.imd.gov.in but every one returned 'Your IP needs to be whitelisted' — registration-gated, unusable from Vercel today, so they are the production/credibility story, not the demo path. The alert thresholds themselves come straight from Indian government documents verified this session: IMD's own bulletin PDF gives Heavy 64.5–115.5 mm, Very Heavy 115.6–204.4 mm, Extremely Heavy >=204.5 mm per 24h, and the official Manual for Drought Management (agriwelfare.gov.in) defines a dry spell as 3–4 consecutive weeks with weekly rainfall <50% of normal after monsoon onset. Embed the thresholds as constants (they are definitions, not data), fetch Open-Meteo live, and cite IMD/DAC&FW for the rules — that combination is demo-safe today and credible to judges.
demo_plan: Single verified endpoint, no signup, call server-side from a Next.js route handler and cache ~1h per zone: GET https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude={lat}&longitude={lon}&daily=precipitation_sum,et0_fao_evapotranspiration,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max&hourly=soil_moisture_0_to_1cm,soil_moisture_3_to_9cm&forecast_days=16&past_days=30&timezone=Asia%2FKolkata . Verified response shape: top-level keys latitude, longitude, elevation, timezone, daily_units, daily, hourly_units, hourly. daily.time is ISO dates; daily.precipitation_sum in mm (sample today: 50.6 mm — live monsoon rain at Bhopal); daily.et0_fao_evapotranspiration in mm (sample 1.56–5.36); daily.temperature_2m_max/min in degC (26.8/24.2); daily.precipitation_probability_max in % (100, 86, 29...). hourly.soil_moisture_0_to_1cm and soil_moisture_3_to_9cm in m3/m3 (samples 0.469, 0.486 — saturated monsoon soil). With past_days=30&forecast_days=16 one call returns 46 daily rows (verified: 2026-06-08 through 2026-07-23), so the alert engine computes: (a) dry-spell = count consecutive trailing days with precipitation_sum < 2.5 mm (IMD rainy-day threshold) and project forward using forecast days; (b) heavy-rain alert = any forecast day with precipitation_sum >= 64.5 mm (IMD 'Heavy'), escalate at 115.6 and 204.5; (c) moisture stress = precipitation_sum minus et0_fao_evapotranspiration running balance. CAVEATS: day 16 can contain trailing nulls (verified — guard with null checks); free tier is non-commercial only (<10k calls/day) which is fine for a hackathon, and data is CC-BY 4.0 — put 'Weather data by Open-Meteo.com' attribution in the footer, which also reads well to judges. Hardcode the IMD/DAC&FW thresholds from embed_data as the rule engine and cite them on the alert cards ('per IMD classification', 'per Manual for Drought Management 2016') for government credibility without government uptime risk.
production_plan: Production should front IMD's official APIs with Open-Meteo (paid commercial tier, ~EUR29/mo) as fallback and for soil-moisture/ET0 which IMD does not serve. IMD path, confirmed this session: the endpoints are real and live — https://city.imd.gov.in/api/cityweather.php?id={station_id} (7-day city forecast), https://mausam.imd.gov.in/api/current_wx_api.php?id={station_id}, https://mausam.imd.gov.in/api/nowcastapi.php?id={n} (3-hr nowcast), and https://mausam.imd.gov.in/api/warnings_district_api.php?id={district_id} (the district-level orange/red warning feed — exactly our zone-alert layer) — but each returned 'Your IP/Domain ... needs to be whitelisted', so access = register via API Setu (directory.apisetu.gov.in/api-collection/imd) / write to IMD for IP whitelisting, then route egress through a static-IP proxy (Vercel functions have no fixed IP; a small VPS or QuotaGuard-style proxy solves it). Realistic timeline: days-to-weeks, government approval loop. Layer 2: IMD Agromet Advisory Services (GKMS) district bulletins — ~700 districts, issued twice weekly (Tue/Fri) by AMFUs/DAMUs as PDFs at imdagrimet.gov.in (URL pattern https://imdagrimet.gov.in/Services/DistrictBulletin.php?state={State}&district={District}&language=English); the site was down (ECONNREFUSED) during this session, which itself is the argument for KisanVaani: production would poll+parse these PDFs when up (Gemini can extract the advisory text) and never depend on them synchronously. data.gov.in rainfall datasets are historical/static (normals, 1901–2017 series), useful for computing 'normal weekly rainfall' baselines needed by the official dry-spell formula — download once offline, do not call at runtime (api.data.gov.in was unreachable from two networks this session).
embed_data: {"source_notes":{"rainfall_intensity":"IMD official classification, verified from IMD MC Ahmedabad state bulletin PDF (mausam.imd.gov.in/ahmedabad/mcdata/state.pdf), mm per 24h","dry_spell_official":"Manual for Drought Management, DAC&FW GoI (agriwelfare.gov.in/Documents/Updated Drought Manual_0.pdf), exact quote verified","rainy_day":"IMD: day with >=2.5 mm rainfall"},"rainy_day_mm":2.5,"dry_day_mm_below":2.5,"rainfall_intensity_mm_per_24h":{"very_light":{"min":0.1,"max":2.4},"light":{"min":2.5,"max":15.5},"moderate":{"min":15.6,"max":64.4},"heavy":{"min":64.5,"max":115.5},"very_heavy":{"min":115.6,"max":204.4},"extremely_heavy":{"min":204.5,"max":null},"exceptionally_heavy":"value near highest ever recorded at/near station for that month"},"heavy_rain_alert_levels":[{"level":"yellow","label":"Heavy rain","threshold_mm":64.5},{"level":"orange","label":"Very heavy rain","threshold_mm":115.6},{"level":"red","label":"Extremely heavy rain","threshold_mm":204.5}],"dry_spell_official_definition":{"quote":"A dry spell is a short period, usually 4 weeks (upto 3 weeks in case of light soils), of low rainfall or no rainfall. Thus, consecutive 3-4 weeks after the due date for the onset of monsoon with rainfall less than 50% of the normal in each of the weeks is defined as a Dry spell.","weeks_normal_soil":4,"weeks_light_soil":3,"weekly_rainfall_pct_of_normal_below":50,"caveat":"recalibrate in regions with SW monsoon rainfall >1400mm","source":"Manual for Drought Management 2016 (in effect from Kharif 2017), DAC&FW"},"engine_dry_spell_heuristic":{"note":"KisanVaani day-counting heuristic derived from IMD rainy-day (>=2.5mm) definition; official trigger above needs weekly normals","dry_day_mm_below":2.5,"tiers":[{"consecutive_dry_days":7,"level":"watch"},{"consecutive_dry_days":14,"level":"warning"},{"consecutive_dry_days":21,"level":"severe"}]},"drought_declaration_context":{"mandatory_triggers":["rainfall_deviation","dry_spell"],"rainfall_deviation_formula":"RFdev = ((RFi - RFn)/RFn)*100, RFn = 30yr normal"}}

findings.source_name: Open-Meteo Forecast API (16-day + 30-day lookback, soil moisture, ET0)
findings.verification_note: Fetched live for lat=23.2,lon=77.08 (Bhopal). Got 16 daily rows (2026-07-08..2026-07-23): daily.precipitation_sum=[50.6,8.4,0.0,0.1,...] mm, daily.et0_fao_evapotranspiration=[1.56,4.53,5.0,...] mm, temperature_2m_max=[26.8,31.2,...] C, precipitation_probability_max=[100,86,29,...]%, hourly.soil_moisture_0_to_1cm=[0.469,...] and soil_moisture_3_to_9cm=[0.486,...] m3/m3 (384 hourly rows). Second call with past_days=30 returned 46 days starting 2026-06-08 with real past values. Note: final forecast day can contain nulls (day-16 precipitation_sum was None) — guard for it. No API key sent.
findings.notes: Terms verified at open-meteo.com/en/terms: free tier keyless, <10,000 calls/day (5,000/hr, 600/min), NON-COMMERCIAL only, data CC-BY 4.0 — must show 'Weather data by Open-Meteo.com' attribution. Fine for hackathon; commercial launch needs their paid API plan.

findings.verification_note: Fetched live: endpoint exists and responds, but body was the literal string 'IP 182.69.180.230 needs to be whitelisted' instead of JSON. Same result for mausam.imd.gov.in/api/current_wx_api.php?id=42667 and /api/nowcastapi.php?id=1 ('Your IP/Domain ... needs to be whitelisted'). API surface is real but gated on IP whitelisting via API Setu/IMD registration.
findings.notes: Vercel serverless has no static egress IP, so even after approval you need a fixed-IP proxy. Do not put in today's demo path; name-drop as the production integration.

findings.source_name: IMD district warnings API (official orange/red alerts)
findings.verification_note: Fetched live: returned 'Your IP 182.69.180.230 needs to be whitelisted' (endpoint exists — a wrong path like /api/districtwise_warning_api.php returns a normal Apache 404 instead). This is the district-level warning feed KisanVaani zone alerts would mirror in production.
findings.notes: Strongest production citation: 'zone alerts will carry official IMD district warnings once whitelisted'. Not usable server-side today.

findings.source_name: IMD Agromet Advisory Services (GKMS) district bulletins
findings.verification_note: imdagrimet.gov.in refused connections from both my machine (curl HTTP 000) and WebFetch infrastructure (ECONNREFUSED 164.100.114.65:443) throughout the session — site down/unreachable. agromet.imd.gov.in resolves but redirects to index.php/login/login_form (internal portal for AMFU bulletin authors). Bulletin URL pattern and PDF path pattern (accessData.php?path=Files/District+AAS+Bulletin/English+Bulletin/...pdf) confirmed via search index only.
findings.notes: ~700 district bulletins issued twice weekly (Tuesday and Friday) by AMFUs/DAMUs as PDFs; per-district crop advisories. Production plan: poll and parse PDFs with Gemini when the site is up; never a synchronous dependency. Its flakiness (down during this session) is itself a talking point for why farmers need an aggregator.

findings.verification_note: api.data.gov.in was unreachable this session from two independent network paths (local curl: empty response/HTTP 000; WebFetch: ECONNREFUSED 164.100.61.198:443), though www.data.gov.in itself returned HTTP 200. Could not retrieve any records with the public sample key.
findings.notes: Even when up, its rainfall holdings are historical/static (district rainfall normals, sub-division monthly series 1901-2017) — useful offline to compute 'normal weekly rainfall' baselines for the official dry-spell formula, not for real-time forecasts. Free API key on signup; sample key 579b464db66ec23bdd000001cdd3946e44ce4aad7209ff7b23ac571b exists for testing. API Setu IMD collection page (directory.apisetu.gov.in/api-collection/imd) is a JS SPA; the underlying APIs are the whitelisted mausam ones above.

findings.source_name: IMD rainfall intensity classification (heavy/very heavy/extremely heavy)
findings.verification_note: Downloaded this official IMD MC Ahmedabad bulletin PDF (HTTP 200, 1.4MB) and extracted the terminology table text verbatim: 'Very light rainfall Trace-2.4 | Light rainfall 2.5-15.5 | Moderate rainfall 15.6-64.4 | Heavy Rainfall 64.5-115.5 | Very Heavy Rainfall 115.6-204.4 | Extremely heavy rainfall Greater than or equal to 204.5 mm | Exceptionally Heavy Rainfall: value near about the highest recorded rainfall at or near the station for the month'. All mm per 24h.
findings.notes: Embed as constants in the alert engine (see embed_data). Cite as 'IMD rainfall classification' on alert cards. Corroborated by IMD brochure 'Heavy Rainfall Warning Services' (mausam.imd.gov.in/imd_latest/contents/pdf/pubbrochures/Heavy%20Rainfall%20Warning%20Services.pdf, image-based PDF, also fetched HTTP 200).

findings.source_name: Dry spell definition — Manual for Drought Management (DAC&FW, Govt of India)
findings.verification_note: Downloaded the official manual (HTTP 200, 5.7MB) and extracted the exact definition text: 'A dry spell is a short period, usually 4 weeks (upto 3 weeks in case of light soils), of low rainfall or no rainfall. Thus, consecutive 3-4 weeks after the due date for the onset of monsoon with rainfall less than 50% of the normal in each of the weeks is defined as a Dry spell.' Plus rainfall deviation trigger formula RFdev={(RFi-RFn)/RFn}*100 with 30-yr normals, and caveat to recalibrate where SW monsoon >1400mm. Dry spell + rainfall deviation are the two mandatory drought triggers.
findings.notes: This is THE citable Indian-government dry-spell definition. IMD 'rainy day' = day with >=2.5mm rain (corroborated: IMD's own light-rainfall class starts at 2.5mm) — use <2.5mm/day as the dry-day counter for the demo heuristic while citing the manual for the official weekly definition.
